paper_title: Did the Bronze Age deforestation of Europe affect its climate? A regional climate model study using pollen-based land cover reconstructions

methods.description: This method involves simulating climate conditions for 2.5 ka and pre-industrial (PI) periods using a chain of model simulations. It uses the EC-Earth global model, LPJ-GUESS dynamic vegetation model, and regional climate models (RCMs) to study the impact of land cover changes on climate.

steps.description: Simulate 2.5 ka and PI climate conditions using the ocean-atmosphere coupled global model EC-Earth with prescribed potential PI land cover.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate climate conditions using EC-Earth model

steps.description: Use 2.5 ka climate conditions to force the DVM LPJ-GUESS over the European domain to simulate potential land cover.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate potential land cover using LPJ-GUESS

steps.description: Use climate conditions from EC-Earth and land cover from LPJ-GUESS to force RCMs RCA4 and HCLIM for high-resolution climate simulation.
outputs: high-resolution climate simulation for 2.5 ka and PI

steps.description: Reconstruct land cover at 2.5 ka using pollen records and the REVEALS model, and interpolate using spatial statistics.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct and interpolate land cover using pollen data

steps.description: Perform PI and 2.5 ka simulations following the PMIP4 protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: perform climate simulations following PMIP4 protocol
